articles.title: U.S. Sanctions New Iranian Agency That Claims Authority Over Strait Of Hormuz
articles.content:
周三，特朗普政府在战争期间实施了新一轮广泛的经济施压措施，对伊朗实施了额外制裁，此次目标是该国新成立的机构——试图控制霍尔木兹海峡航运的波斯湾海峡管理局。

据一些不愿公开身份、匿名发言的美国官员称，这些制裁是在周三晚些时候宣布的，此前美军对一处伊朗军事设施实施了打击，以回应伊朗无人机被击落事件。

据美联社最先报道，这是美国最近一次利用经济手段施压伊朗的举措，旨在通过经济杠杆配合军事行动，迫使伊朗领导层达成协议，结束战争并开放这条全球五分之一石油和天然气运输必经的水道。特朗普总统曾表示协议即将达成，但目前仍在谈判中。

“伊朗军方最近试图勒索全球海运贸易的行为，证明经济制裁已令该政权陷入财政困境，”财政部长斯科特·贝森特在一份声明中表示。

此次制裁针对的是伊朗新近成立的波斯湾海峡管理局，以及任何与该机构合作的个人或实体。该机构于本月早些时候宣布成立，负责批准船舶通过海峡并收取通行费，每艘船的费用可能高达200万美元。

伊朗强大的准军事组织革命卫队为这一监管举措进行了辩护，称其指定的航道是穿越这一关键水道的唯一安全路线，任何偏离该路线的船只都将面临一系列攻击和风险。

伊朗对海峡的控制已引发全球能源市场的震荡。此次封锁发生在2月28日美国和以色列发起战争之后。油价、天然气价格及相关产品价格均大幅上涨。专家表示，一旦水道重新开放，航运和价格的恢复可能需要数周甚至数月时间。

作为回应，美国已对伊朗港口实施了一个多月的封锁。特朗普表示，“封锁将一直持续，直到达成协议、经过认证并正式签署。”

在华盛顿与德黑兰进行近年来最激烈外交谈判之际，这些最新的经济制裁措施出台。双方正努力结束战争，并寻求长期解决这两个长期对手之间问题的路径。

特朗普周三表示，伊朗“正在用尽最后的谈判筹码”，并称尽管美国军方周一表示对导弹发射阵地和布雷船只实施了“自卫”打击，双方仍接近达成协议。周三的打击行动可能会带来更多复杂因素。

特朗普在周三的内阁会议上表示：“他们非常希望达成协议。到目前为止，他们还没能做到。我们对此并不满意，但我们会满意的——要么达成协议，要么我们就必须完成任务。”

这位共和党总统还重申了他的警告，即如果无法达成协议，战争将重新开始，但在过去几个月中，他已经多次收回此类威胁。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

The Trump administration on Wednesday placed additional sanctions on Iran as part of a sprawling economic pressure campaign during the war, this time targeting the country’s newly created agency that is trying to control shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

The sanctions were announced late Wednesday after U.S. forces carried out strikes on an Iran military facility after downing Iranian attack drones, according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to comment publicly and spoke on the condition of anonymity.

Advertisement

The sanctions move, first reported by The Associated Press, is the latest U.S. effort to use economic leverage on top of military action to push Iran’s leadership into an agreement to end the war and open the waterway where a fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas normally passes. President Donald Trump has said a deal is imminent, but talks are ongoing.

Rising energy prices and other costs stemming from Iran’s effective closure of the strait have heaped political pressure on Trump and other Republicans ahead of the midterm congressional elections.

“The Iranian military’s latest attempt to extort global maritime trade is proof that Economic Fury has left the regime desperate for cash,” Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a statement.

Advertisement

The sanctions target Iran’s Persian Gulf Strait Authority and any person or entity cooperating with the agency, announced earlier this month, that approves transit in the strait and charges tolls that could reach as high as $2 million per vessel.

Iran’s powerful paramilitary Revolutionary Guard has defended this oversight effort, saying the only safe route for transit through the critical waterway is through the corridor it has designated and saying any ships that deviate from that path face a series of attacks and risks.

Advertisement

Iran’s chokehold on the strait has caused worldwide energy shocks and followed the U.S. and Israel launching the war on Feb. 28. Prices have spiked for oil, gas and related products, and experts say it would take several weeks or even months for shipping and prices to recover once the waterway reopens.

In turn, the U.S. has blockaded Iranian ports for over a month, and Trump said it “will remain in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified, and signed.”

&
Advertisement

The latest economic penalties come as Washington and Tehran have been engaged in some of the most intense diplomacy and negotiations in years, aimed at bringing an end to the war and a pathway for a longer-term solution to issues between the longtime adversaries.

Trump said Wednesday that Iran is “negotiating on fumes” and said the sides are closing in on a deal even after the U.S. military said Monday that it had carried out “self-defense” strikes on missile launch sites and boats placing mines. Wednesday’s strikes are likely to cause more complications.

“They want very much to make a deal,” Trump said at a Cabinet meeting Wednesday. “So far, they haven’t gotten there. We’re not satisfied with it, but we will be — either that or we’ll have to just finish the job.”

Advertisement

The Republican president also has reiterated his warning that fighting would resume if no deal is reached but has pulled back from those threats several times now in the last few months.

Related
Iran warStrait of HormuzPersian Gulf Strait Authority

articles.title: U.S. Carries Out New Strikes In Iran Against Military Site, Official Says
articles.content:
华盛顿5月27日（路透社）——一位美国官员周三对路透社表示，美军夜间在伊朗实施了新的打击，目标是一处军事设施，美方官员认为该设施对美国军队和霍尔木兹海峡的商业航运构成威胁。

这位匿名官员表示，美军还拦截并击落了多架具有类似威胁的伊朗无人机。

这些美军打击行动此前未曾报道，正值各方谈判结束一场持续三个月之久的战争之际。这场战争自2月28日美国和以色列发动攻击以来，已造成数千人死亡，并导致全球能源价格大幅上涨。

美国总统特朗普周三早些时候驳回了伊朗国家媒体关于伊朗和阿曼将共同管理霍尔木兹海峡航运的报道，称其为和平协议的一部分。特朗普表示，该海峡将保持开放。

美国上次于周一实施了所称的防御性打击，伊朗称此举违反了两国脆弱的停火协议。美军打击目标包括试图布设水雷的船只和导弹发射阵地，美国军方中央司令部称这些目标对美国军队构成威胁。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

WASHINGTON, May 27 (Reuters) - The U.S. military carried out new strikes overnight in Iran targeting a military site that officials believed posed a threat to U.S. forces and commercial maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a U.S. official told Reuters on Wednesday.

The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, said the U.S. military has also intercepted and shot down multiple Iranian drones that posed a similar threat.

Advertisement

The U.S. military strikes, which have not been previously reported, came during negotiations to end a three-month-old war that has killed thousands and sent global energy prices sharply higher since it began on February 28 with U.S. and Israeli attacks.

U.S. President Donald Trump earlier on Wednesday dismissed a Iranian state media report that Iran and Oman would jointly manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as part of a peace deal. Trump said the waterway would remain open.

The U.S. last carried out what it called defensive strikes against Iran on Monday, in what Iran called a violation of the countries’ fragile ceasefire. The U.S. targets included boats attempting to lay mines and missile launch sites that the U.S. military’s Central Command said posed a threat to U.S. forces.

Advertisement
Related

articles.title: Haitian Soccer Player Still Awaits U.S. Visa As Trump Travel Ban Disrupts World Cup Plans
articles.content:
圣胡安，波多黎各（美联社）——海地国家足球队中唯一一位留在动荡不安的加勒比海国家效力的球员，正在等待美国签证，以便前往佛罗里达与球队会合，而世界杯开赛仅剩数周时间。

伍登斯基·皮埃尔是一名防守型中场，效力于海地首都太子港的Violette AC俱乐部。据估计，该市约70%的区域正被暴力团伙围困。

这支世界杯阵容中的其他25名球员均在海外联赛效力，包括英格兰、法国、葡萄牙、加拿大和美国等国家和地区。

本届世界杯将是海地历史上第二次亮相世界杯赛场——该国上一次也是唯一一次登上这一足球最高舞台是在1974年。

海地足球联合会发言人西厄·让蒂表示，1974年世界杯阵容中除一名球员外，其余球员当时都在海地生活和踢球。

皮埃尔以及近十名海地足球联合会官员的美国签证尚未获批。此前，美国特朗普政府继续扩大对包括海地在内等国家的旅行限制。

皮埃尔在太子港高档社区佩蒂翁维尔的一个人工草皮球场继续训练，等待签证，“希望尽快与球队会合”，让蒂周三在电话采访中告诉美联社。

“我希望他世界杯后不必继续住在（海地），”让蒂说。“这是他向世界展示自己的机会，也是等待合同的机会。”

球队在太子港的主场——距离皮埃尔的家乡不远——因过于危险而被放弃，海地被迫在库拉索岛进行“主场”世界杯预选赛。

皮埃尔来自太阳城，这是太子港的一个海滨贫民窟，多年来饱受暴力和饥饿之苦，也是强大帮派头目的据点。太阳城曾发生多起大屠杀和帮派性强奸事件，最近的暴力活动已导致超过5300人流离失所，这是联合国国际移民组织最新报告中的数据。

“他能参加世界杯是一种骄傲，”让蒂谈及皮埃尔时说。“这对（足球）官员和公众来说都令人满意。正如你所知，海地正处于危机之中。”

皮埃尔未回复置评请求，球队教练也未回应。

海地队官员于周日抵达佛罗里达，球员们于周二在圣卢西亚港开始训练。让蒂表示，另有三名球员计划于周三抵达。

皮埃尔的情况仍有时间解决。海地将在南佛罗里达进行两场世界杯热身赛，首先是周二对阵新西兰，随后是6月5日对阵秘鲁。

海地将于6月13日在马萨诸塞州福克斯伯勒对阵苏格兰，揭开世界杯序幕，随后于6月19日在费城对阵五届冠军巴西，6月24日在亚特兰大对阵摩洛哥。

让蒂表示，他于2022年首次与皮埃尔接触，当时他们一起前往洪都拉斯参加一场U20比赛。

“我认为他是一名顶级球员，”让蒂说，并补充道“每个人都非常高兴”皮埃尔能加入球队。

“海地有足球，”让蒂说。“这是一个想要活下去的国家。”

——

美联社迈阿密体育记者蒂姆·雷诺兹对此报道有贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The only member of Haiti’s national soccer team who is based in the troubled Caribbean country is awaiting a U.S. visa to join the squad in Florida just weeks before the start of the World Cup.

Woodensky Pierre is a defensive midfielder who plays for Violette AC in Haiti’s capital, Port-au-Prince. An estimated 70% of that city is under siege by violent gangs.

Advertisement

The other 25 players on the World Cup squad play outside the country, coming from leagues in places like England, France, Portugal, Canada and the United States.

This year’s World Cup will be only the second for Haiti in its history — the country’s only other appearance on soccer’s biggest stage was in 1974.

Haitian soccer federation spokesman Thecieux Jeanty said all but one player on the 1974 World Cup team was living and playing in Haiti at that time.

Advertisement

The U.S. visas for Pierre and nearly a dozen other officials with the Haitian soccer federation have yet to come through as the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump continues to expand travel restrictions from countries including Haiti.

Pierre continues to train at a field with synthetic grass in Pétion-Ville, an upscale area of Port-au-Prince, as he awaits his visa, “in hopes of being with his team as soon as possible,” Jeanty told The Associated Press in a phone interview Wednesday.

“I hope he doesn’t have to live in (Haiti) after the World Cup,” Jeanty said. “It’s an opportunity to present himself to the world and await a contract.”

Advertisement

The team’s home stadium in Port-au-Prince — not far from the neighborhood where Pierre is from — was deemed too dangerous, forcing Haiti to play its “home” World Cup qualifiers in Curaçao.

Pierre is from Cite Soleil, a seaside slum that has endured violence and hunger for years and serves as a base for powerful gang leaders. Cite Soleil has seen multiple massacres and gang rapes, with violence recently displacing more than 5,300 people, according to the latest report from the U.N.’s International Organization for Migration.

Advertisement

“It’s a source of pride for him,” Jeanty said of Pierre being able to play in the World Cup. “It’s satisfying for (soccer) officials and for the public, too. As you know, Haiti is in crisis.”

Pierre did not return messages seeking comment, and neither did the team’s coach.

Officials for Haiti’s squad arrived in Florida on Sunday, and players began training in Port St. Lucie on Tuesday. Three other players are scheduled to arrive on Wednesday, Jeanty said.

There is still time to resolve Pierre’s situation. Haiti will play two World Cup warmup matches, first against New Zealand on Tuesday and then against Peru on June 5, in South Florida.

Advertisement

Haiti opens World Cup play on June 13 in Foxborough, Massachusetts, against Scotland, then plays five-time champion Brazil on June 19 in Philadelphia and Morocco on June 24 in Atlanta.

Jeanty said he first made contact with Pierre in 2022, when they traveled together to Honduras for a under-20 match.

“I saw him as a top-level player,” Jeanty said, adding that “everybody’s very happy” to have Pierre on the team.

Advertisement

“There is soccer in Haiti,” Jeanty said. “It’s a country that wants to live.”

___

AP Sports Writer Tim Reynolds in Miami contributed to this report.

___

AP World Cup: https://apnews.com/hub/fifa-world-cup
